action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 24's inspection script crashed with CUDA OOM before yielding useful compositing information. We still have a baseline LPIPS of 0.5692 and no transparent backgrounds in GT. Analysis: The most likely cause of the poor PSNR (~6) is a background-color mismatch between our renders and GT. Our model.py either uses raw rgb (uncomposited) or comp_rgb with an unknown background color, then slaps on alpha=255. If GT has white backgrounds but our empty pixels are black, LPIPS will be terrible. We need to know: (1) the renderer's bg_color from config, (2) the GT border color, and (3) exactly what the internal renderer returns (comp_rgb vs rgb+opacity). Decision: Write a lightweight, single-object inspection script that avoids OOM by not loading LPIPS and only rendering 1 view. Run it to gather these facts. Then in the next iteration we can fix model.py and potentially start fine-tuning.
